title: Centratherum repens
intro: Centratherum repens, also known as creeping centratherum or creeping plant, is a low-growing, spreading herb native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Centratherum repens include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Centratherum repens is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health ailments.
taxonomy: Centratherum repens is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Heliantheae and the genus Centratherum. The genus Centratherum is classified under the subfamily Asteroideae. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Its classification reflects its close relationship to other plants in the aster family.
uses: Centratherum repens has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, where it is valued for its various health benefits. The plant is often employed to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In addition, Centratherum repens has been used to reduce inflammation and to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues. It is also believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Centratherum repens.

The flavonoids present in Centratherum repens may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.
